pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly creates and targets the 'ecommerce' namespace for all deployments. verifier.py strictly expects the namespace to be 'ecommerce'.
setup_task.sh explicitly injects exactly 4 separate deployment failures.
setup_task.sh creates deployments and services specifically named api-gateway, web-frontend, cache-layer, and batch-processor.

metadata_value: api-gateway uses a non-existent Docker image tag causing ImagePullBackOff
web-frontend Service selector does not match pod labels causing zero endpoints
cache-layer ConfigMap has wrong Redis port (6380 instead of 6379)
batch-processor has excessive memory request (32Gi) causing Pending state
verified_value: api-gateway uses a non-existent Docker image tag causing ImagePullBackOff
web-frontend Service selector does not match pod labels causing zero endpoints
cache-layer ConfigMap has wrong Redis port (6380 instead of 6379)
batch-processor has excessive memory request (32Gi) causing Pending state
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh defines api-gateway with image 'nginx:broken-tag-xyz-nonexistent', web-frontend service selector 'app: web-frontend' against pod label 'app: frontend-app', cache-config ConfigMap REDIS_PORT='6380', and batch-processor memory request '32Gi'.
verifier.py defines PASS_THRESHOLD = 70 and uses it to determine task success.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated Kubernetes resources in the 'ecommerce' namespace. It has been verified via the setup_task.sh script that exactly 4 microservices are injected with failures: 'api-gateway' uses a non-existent image ('nginx:broken-tag-xyz-nonexistent'), 'web-frontend' has a service selector mismatch ('app: frontend-app' pod labels vs 'app: web-frontend' selector), 'cache-layer' ConfigMap specifies an incorrect Redis port (6380 instead of 6379), and 'batch-processor' requests excessive memory ('32Gi') causing a Pending state. The verifier strictly enforces a minimum passing score of 70 points.
